summary: finup-mongo-library initializes a hardcoded Sentry DSN at import time, routing consumer app errors and profiles to the author
details: The package advertises itself as a MongoDB schemas/DTOs library for NestJS, but `dist/common/instrument.js` calls `Sentry.init()` at module top level with a hardcoded DSN (`https://969cb6a5b1971c24320e1d488a2e8d3e@o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920`) and `tracesSampleRate: 1.0`, `profilesSampleRate: 1.0`. This file is re-exported from `dist/index.js`, so any consumer that `require()`s `finup-mongo-library` immediately initializes the process-global Sentry SDK pointing at the author's ingest endpoint. From then on, every uncaught exception, stack trace, performance span, and CPU profile in the consumer's Node.js process is silently shipped to a third party unrelated to the library's stated purpose. Error and profile payloads commonly contain request paths, query parameters, environment metadata, and source-code context, so this is a one-way data flow from the consumer's production app to the author. It also clobbers the consumer's own Sentry configuration because Sentry maintains a single global hub per process. The behavior is undisclosed in the package's stated functionality and fires automatically on import with no opt-out.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
